title: Fermented Slovakian Basil Horapa Brunch Dish
description: Light and refreshing Slovakian-inspired brunch dish with a unique Thai-sweet-basil-horapa flavor profile. Serve at room temperature for a delightful brunch experience.
instructions: Combine kefir, rice vinegar, sugar, Slovakian fresh horseradish, Thai basil, horapa leaves, salt, and water in a large bowl, whisking until the sugar dissolves.
Add pumpernickel bread croutons to the bowl and stir to combine.

In a separate bowl, mix together sour cream, chopped fresh dill, sesame seeds, chopped fresh chives, grated ginger, honey, lemon juice, egg yolk, smoked paprika, black pepper, garlic powder, cayenne pepper, and fresh parsley.
Add the sour cream mixture to the kefir mixture, stirring to combine.
Heat 1 tablespoon of olive oil in a small skillet over low heat.
Add rapini to the skillet and cook, stirring occasionally, until tender, about 5 minutes.
Stir in fennel seeds and cook for 1 minute.
Add the cooked rapini mixture to the kefir mixture, stirring to combine.
Cover the bowl with plastic wrap and refrigerate for at least 2 hours or overnight to allow the flavors to meld.
To serve, transfer the fermented Slovakian basil horapa brunch dish to a serving bowl and garnish with additional chopped fresh herbs, if desired.
Serve at room temperature for a delightful brunch experience.
Optional: garnish with additional horapa leaves or Thai basil for added flavor and color.
Optional: serve with toasted pumpernickel bread or crackers for a crunchy texture.
Optional: add a dollop of sour cream on top for extra creaminess.
Optional: sprinkle with chopped fresh chives or parsley for added flavor and color.
Optional: serve with a side of steamed vegetables or a simple green salad for a well-rounded meal.
Optional: garnish with a fried or poached egg for added protein.
Optional: serve with a side of grilled meats or roasted vegetables for added protein and flavor.
Optional: garnish with a sprinkle of smoked paprika or chopped fresh herbs for added flavor and color.
Optional: serve with a side of pickled vegetables or a simple salad for a tangy and refreshing side dish.
Optional: garnish with a dollop of sour cream or a sprinkle of chopped fresh herbs for added creaminess and flavor.
